text: This is a laparoscopic duodenal atresia repair with a diamond-shaped anastomosis. The patient is a two-day-old boy born at full term with a prenatal diagnosis of double bowel. The diagnosis was confirmed after birth with an x-ray. He had an NG tube to decompress the stomach, and he was planned to take to the operating room for a laparoscopic repair. His cardiac echo was normal. This was our port placement.

The surgeon identifies the anatomy, with the ligament atrites visible to the left of the midline. Congenital adhesions are dissected using a combination of blunt dissection and electrocautery. A transcutaneous suture suspends the gallbladder from the anterior abdominal wall.

The surgeon suspends the proximal duodenum while taking down normal adhesions to better identify the anatomy. The distal duodenum is skeletonized using blunt dissection and electrocautery. The proximal duodenum appears large and distended on the right side of the screen, the pancreas is visible in the middle, and the small decompressed distal duodenum is on the left. A transverse duodenotomy is created using electrocautery and sharp scissors, followed by a distal longitudinal duodenotomy.

The surgeon aligns the duodenotomies using a transcutaneous suture to create a diamond-shaped anastomosis. Gentle manipulation is applied to ensure proper alignment, and the anastomosis begins with suturing the duodenotomies closer to the camera, progressing away from the camera view.

The surgeon completes the posterior wall of the diamond-shaped anastomosis in a continuous manner, incorporating locking sutures every two to three steps. The transcutaneous suture stabilizes the anastomosis, allowing the surgeon to suture away from their position with ease.

The surgeon advances the NG tube into the stomach and further into the distal duodenum, confirming the patency of the duodenum. The tube is flushed with warm normal saline. The anterior part of the anastomosis is then completed using continuous suturing with locking sutures every two to three steps.
And voila, the diamond shape anastomosis. were started on post of day five and he was finally discharged home post of day fourteen
